text:
Nancy picked up the newspaper from the doorstep. As she settled down to read, her eyes fell on a small, bold print headline: "The Great Snowball Fight of Winter." Intrigued, she tucked the paper into her coat pocket.

A week later, the town was blanketed in snow, and the streets were a swirl of playful children. Nancy saw an opportunity and pulled out the newspaper. Armed with three snowy balls of various sizes, she positioned herself on the sidewalk with a grin.

Across the street, Chuck, known for his snowball tactics, spotted the unsuspecting Nancy. Her face lit up with mischief in the bright sunlight. With a gentle toss, he hurled the small ball forward. Nancy deflected it with her stick, then countered with her own.

The snowball fight escalated, snow flying in all directions, ice skating skills on full display as folks from near and far joined in. Nancy's strength made the largest snowball of them all, and she lob
